Patch Set 4:

Just a question... Is there any reason it used Groovy for all the business logic, instead of Java logic? In SO, we try to get rid of Groovy dependencies.

Patch Set 4: Code-Review+2

For now, I approve it. But, in Honolulu, we need to discuss use of Groovy. The reason we switched from Groovy to Java is to increase code coverage.
Change has been successfully merged by Byung-Woo Jun

Patch Set 4:

> Patch Set 4:
> 
> Just a question... Is there any reason it used Groovy for all the business logic, instead of Java logic? In SO, we try to get rid of Groovy dependencies.

Hi Byung-Woo Jun,
In SO, we have a groovy script corresponding to every bpmn process since its inception. Not sure of the reason but if we want to completely move to java, I suppose there will be one java delegate per block in the bpmn process which might be difficult to maintain.

Patch Set 4:

@Priyadharshini,the reason the SO team migrated Groovy code to Java was to increase automated code coverage. At that time, we did not find an automated way for Groovy. As you may know, SO Groovy code was not include and reduced the overall SO code coverage. I remember it correctly, that was one of the major reasons. For that, AT&T SO team invented a way to call Java code from the service task. Since then, the SO team follow this code practice. That is why I told you that the SO team needs to discuss for Honolulu. Since the amount of Groovy code you have put is a lot. I think it is too late for Guilin release to change the course.

As you may know, SO Groovy code was not include and reduced the overall SO code coverage. I remember it correctly, that was one of the major reasons. For that, AT&T SO team invented a way to call Java code from the service task. Since then, the SO team follow this code practice. That is why I told you that the SO team needs to discuss for Honolulu. Since the amount of Groovy code you have put is a lot. I think it is too late for Guilin release to change the course.

Sure, We are not aware of the new way. So,may be we can discuss that in the SO call and follow the same from Honolulu.
